meta_title: For Students: analytics setup to Solve no version control (Intermediate Checklist)
meta_description: Learn how to analytics setup and overcome no version control. A Intermediate-level Checklist using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Students: Analytics Setup to Solve No Version Control
content.seo_description: Stop flying blind. Learn how to implement robust analytics to track your app's health and replace the chaos of no version control with data-driven insights.
content.answer_block: Without version control, your code is a ticking time bomb. By integrating telemetry and analytics, you gain observability into how your app behaves in production. This allows you to track errors and user journeys, providing a safety net that maps your development progress even when you aren't using Git.
content.intro:
You’re building your first major project, but there’s a silence in your workflow—a lack of accountability. Without version control, every save is a gamble, and every bug is a ghost you can’t trace. You are building in the dark, hoping your code holds up under the pressure of real-world inputs.

But what if you could see through the fog? By instrumenting your application with high-fidelity analytics, you transform your project from a fragile script into a observable system. This isn't just about tracking users; it's about building a telemetry layer that tells you exactly where your code breaks, why it fails, and how to fix it before your users notice.

steps.title: Define Your Telemetry Schema
steps.description: Before writing code, map out the critical user events and error states you need to track to identify performance regressions.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a telemetry schema for a React SaaS app that tracks user signups, feature usage, and unhandled promise rejections.
steps.claude_code_command: claude ask 'Generate a telemetry.ts file that defines event types for my app usage'

steps.title: Inject Analytics Middleware
steps.description: Integrate a lightweight analytics provider to capture lifecycle events automatically.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a clean middleware function to intercept API requests and log status codes to an external analytics endpoint.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Add analytics middleware to the existing API route handlers'

steps.title: Automate Error Boundary Logging
steps.description: Capture unhandled exceptions to gain visibility into your app's fragility when you lack versioned rollback capabilities.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a robust error boundary component that sends stack traces to my analytics dashboard.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Implement global error boundary with logging to analytics'

steps.title: Setup Automated Health Dashboards
steps.description: Visualize your data to spot patterns in failure, effectively creating a 'version history' based on system behavior.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a configuration file for a dashboard that visualizes error spikes over time.
steps.claude_code_command: claude ask 'How do I query my telemetry data to find the most frequent app crashes?'

steps.title: Verify Data Integrity
steps.description: Run a simulation to ensure your events are firing correctly before moving to the next feature iteration.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a test script to simulate 50 user clicks and verify the analytics payload delivery.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Create a test file to verify analytics event triggering'

pro_tips: Always log the 'previous state' alongside an error to simulate manual version tracking.
Use sampling to reduce costs; you don't need 100% of successful events, but you do need 100% of errors.
Add a 'correlation ID' to every request to trace a single user session across multiple service calls.
Instrument your local development environment to catch bugs before they ever reach production.
Treat your analytics dashboard as your primary 'source of truth' regarding the stability of your current build.
content.next_steps: Once your analytics are live, use the data to identify your most frequent crash point and refactor that specific module to be idempotent.
content.conversion_block: You now have the visibility to build with the confidence of a senior engineer. Stop guessing, start measuring, and turn your first project into a portfolio-grade success. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
